Scenario: Verify user can connect to BIM 360 as source
Steps:
1. Go to New Migration
2. Select BIM 360 as source
3. Enter valid credentials
4. Click Connect
ExpectedResult: Connection successful and project list loads

Scenario: Verify user can connect to Egnyte as source
Steps:
1. Go to New Migration
2. Select Egnyte as source
3. Enter valid credentials
4. Click Connect
ExpectedResult: Connection successful and folder list loads

Scenario: Verify error shown for invalid source credentials
Steps:
1. Enter wrong credentials for source
2. Click Connect
ExpectedResult: Clear error message shown — connection not established

Scenario: Verify source connection supports token refresh on expiry
Steps:
1. Let token expire
2. Attempt migration
ExpectedResult: Token refreshed automatically — no manual re-auth required

Scenario: Verify user can connect to destination successfully
Steps:
1. Select destination type
2. Enter valid credentials
3. Click Connect
ExpectedResult: Destination connected — folder/bucket list loads

Scenario: Verify error shown for invalid destination credentials
Steps:
1. Enter wrong destination credentials
2. Click Connect
ExpectedResult: Clear error message — connection not established

Scenario: Verify destination path can be selected or created
Steps:
1. Connect to destination
2. Browse or create target folder/bucket
ExpectedResult: Target path selectable and saved correctly

Scenario: Verify user can select specific folders to migrate
Steps:
1. Connect source
2. Browse folder tree
3. Select specific folders
ExpectedResult: Only selected folders included in migration scope

Scenario: Verify Select All selects all folders
Steps: 1. Click Select All in folder tree
ExpectedResult: All folders marked as selected

Scenario: Verify deselecting a folder excludes it from migration
Steps:
1. Select all
2. Deselect one folder
3. Run migration
ExpectedResult: Deselected folder absent from migration results

Scenario: Verify folder count shown matches actual count
Steps:
1. Select folders
2. Note count shown in UI
3. Compare to actual folder count
ExpectedResult: Displayed count matches actual selected folders

Scenario: Verify migration starts when Start is clicked
Steps:
1. Configure source and destination
2. Click Start Migration
ExpectedResult: Migration starts — status shows Running

Scenario: Verify migration status shows Running during execution
Steps:
1. Start a migration
2. Observe status column
ExpectedResult: Status = Running while migration is active

Scenario: Verify migration completes with status Completed
Steps:
1. Run a full migration
2. Wait for completion
ExpectedResult: Status changes to Completed

Scenario: Verify Completed with Errors status shown when some files fail
Steps: 1. Run migration with some inaccessible files
ExpectedResult: Status = Completed with Errors — failed files listed

Scenario: Verify migration can be paused and resumed
Steps:
1. Start migration
2. Click Pause
3. Click Resume
ExpectedResult: Migration pauses and resumes from where it stopped

Scenario: Verify migration can be cancelled
Steps:
1. Start migration
2. Click Cancel/Stop
ExpectedResult: Migration stops — status updated accordingly

Scenario: Verify delta migration runs only for changed files
Steps:
1. Run full migration
2. Modify some source files
3. Run delta migration
ExpectedResult: Only modified/new files included in delta run

Scenario: Verify migration report is generated after completion
Steps:
1. Complete a migration
2. Navigate to Reports
ExpectedResult: Report entry visible with correct source and target

Scenario: Verify report shows correct file counts
Steps:
1. Complete migration with known file count
2. Open report
ExpectedResult: Total/Success/Failed counts match actual migration results

Scenario: Verify report can be downloaded as CSV
Steps:
1. Open migration report
2. Click Download
ExpectedResult: CSV file downloads with correct data

Scenario: Verify failed files listed in report with error reason
Steps:
1. Run migration with some failures
2. Open report details
ExpectedResult: Each failed file shows error/reason
